context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T06:24Z after extensibility/multi-surface slices. Branch develop is clean and ahead of origin/develop by 12 local commits. Last pushed remote baseline remains origin/develop at 3f512fae. Disk remains constrained but stable at about 7.6GB free; repo itself is about 1.5GB, with node_modules about 975MB, so no cleanup was run because it would save little and increase rebuild cost.
current_tasks: Completed atomic extensibility slices since prior handoff: docs multi-surface model, plugin-manifest validation/helpers, Homestead surface mounting, real stream UI subscriber, multi-surface example, and example conformance coverage.

Local HEAD at handoff: a626c482 test(plugin-manifest): cover multi-surface example. Recent local commits ahead of origin include 2329c56d docs(examples): add multi-surface plugin manifest, 272c42eb refactor(homestead): extract surface slot resolver, 9d2dea87 docs(extensibility): document surface discovery path, 5999170d refactor(homestead): use shared surface helpers, 3a829d4f feat(plugin-manifest): expose extension surface helpers, 750c6af6 feat(homestead): mount declared extension surfaces, 3258e185 docs(streaming): record homestead stream subscriber, a9b9b5c3 feat(homestead): render live stream observations, 22136a13 fix(tractor-ts): notify typed node subscribers, and 8fc9fab9 feat(plugin-manifest): validate extension surfaces.
Plugin manifest now models optional `extensions.surfaces` with layers `tractor`, `homestead`, `pi`, `automation`, `desktop`, and `asset`; validator rejects malformed/duplicate layer-id pairs.

Plugin manifest exports host-neutral helpers: `getExtensionSurfaces(...)`, `extensionSurfaceKey(...)`, `isExtensionSurfaceLayer(...)`, and `EXTENSION_SURFACE_LAYERS`. Tests cover helper behavior and the multi-surface example manifest.
Tractor TS now emits `storage:node:written` after `storeNode(...)`, making `Tractor.onNode(type, handler)` actually reactive for stored graph nodes.

Homestead `StudioShell` now subscribes to `StreamSession` and `StreamChunk` via `Tractor.onNode(...)`, reduces them with Tractor stream helpers, and renders a compact live stream statusbar pill. BrowserSyncClient remains schema-neutral.

Homestead now mounts manifest-declared `homestead` extension surfaces into shell slots while preserving legacy `ui.slots`; slot resolution was extracted to `resolveHomesteadSurfaceSlots(...)` and exported from `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk`.
Added `examples/multi-surface-plugin/` with a valid manifest declaring `homestead`, `asset`, and `automation` surfaces; plugin-manifest tests now validate this example stays conformant.

Validation passed in this stream: `npm --prefix packages/plugin-manifest run test`, `npm --prefix packages/plugin-manifest run test:conformance`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead test`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run type-check`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build`, `npm --prefix packages/tractor-ts run test:unit -- telemetry.test.ts`, `npm --prefix packages/tractor-ts run type-check`, `npm --prefix packages/tractor-ts run build`, `npm run project:validate --silent`, and `git diff --check`.
blockers: Disk is still 99% used on the host mount with about 7.6GB free. The repo is only about 1.5GB and derived artifact totals are low; avoid unnecessary cleanups unless free space falls near 2-3GB or new large artifacts appear.
Rust target dirs were previously removed by clean:heavy, so broad Rust checks will rebuild from scratch and may consume disk. Use scoped Rust commands from AGENTS.md only when Rust is touched.

Full tractor-ts unit suite remains known-noisy when WASM fixtures are missing after cleanup. Prefer focused tests (`telemetry.test.ts`, stream helper tests, or `npm run agent:streaming:clients`) unless intentionally rebuilding fixtures.
pi-lens/LSP may warn or crash on Markdown writes; re-read files after auto-format/fix warnings and prefer direct read/edit for docs.

Push the 12 local commits to origin/develop when ready, then watch CI with `GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 gh run watch --repo aretw0/refarm --exit-status` or list runs first if needed.
Next technical slice: promote the Homestead stream statusbar pill into a richer `homestead` extension surface/panel using the declared `stream-panel` model from the multi-surface example.
Next contract slice: add host-side capability checks for `extensions.surfaces[].capabilities` before mounting/activating richer surfaces; keep discovery separate from activation.
Next daily-driver slice: map `docs/DAILY_DRIVER_PARITY.md` rows to concrete surfaces/commands now that stream output and manifest surfaces have a working path.
Run `npm run clean:light --silent` only at checkpoint boundaries if `.turbo` or Rust incremental artifacts reappear; use `clean:heavy` only near critical disk pressure.

Should `ui.slots` be formally deprecated in favor of `extensions.surfaces` after enough hosts consume the new contract?
Should stream observation UI stay in Homestead shell, move to a plugin-provided panel, or support both shell status and panel views?
